title: Dead pytest config: asyncio_mode='auto' set but pytest-asyncio is not a dependency (emits PytestConfigWarning on every test run)
summary: pyproject.toml's [tool.pytest.ini_options] sets `asyncio_mode = "auto"`, but pytest-asyncio is not declared in dependencies or the dev optional-dependencies, and it is absent from uv.lock. There are no async tests in the suite. As a result, every `make unit` run prints: 'PytestConfigWarning: Unknown config option: asyncio_mode'. The option is dead configuration and should be removed (or, if async tests are intended later, pytest-asyncio should be added to dev deps).
why_good_first_issue: Single-line deletion in pyproject.toml with an immediately observable before/after (the warning disappears). No code logic involved; teaches the contributor how the test config and dependency groups fit together. Zero risk: no async tests exist.
repro_steps: 1) grep -in 'asyncio' pyproject.toml uv.lock -> only pyproject.toml [tool.pytest.ini_options] sets asyncio_mode; pytest-asyncio NOT in uv.lock. 2) On the VM: cd ~/work/observability-charm-tools && export PATH=$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH && make unit.
repro_result: REPRODUCED. `make unit` => 16 passed, 1 warning, with the warnings summary showing: "PytestConfigWarning: Unknown config option: asyncio_mode" (raised from _pytest/config/__init__.py:1441). grep confirms pytest-asyncio is absent from both pyproject.toml deps and uv.lock; no async tests exist in tests/unit/.
fix_hint: Remove the line `asyncio_mode = "auto"` from [tool.pytest.ini_options] in pyproject.toml. (Alternative, only if async tests are planned: add `pytest-asyncio` to [project.optional-dependencies].dev and refresh uv.lock.) Re-run `make unit` to confirm the warning is gone.
notes: Toolchain: this repo uses a Makefile (no tox.ini). Unit env builds cleanly: `make unit` => 16 passed, 100% coverage. `make lint` => all checks passed, 6 files already formatted. No outbound writes performed.

title: Unused tool config in pyproject.toml: [tool.black] and [tool.codespell] are configured but neither tool is a dependency or run anywhere (ruff is the formatter)
summary: pyproject.toml declares [tool.black] (line 34, line-length/target-version) and [tool.codespell] (line 61, skip list), but neither 'black' nor 'codespell' appears in the project/dev dependencies or in uv.lock, and neither is invoked by the Makefile (lock/lint/static/fmt/unit) nor by .github/workflows/pull_request.yaml (which only runs make static, make lint, make unit). Formatting/linting is entirely handled by ruff. The black/codespell sections are stale/misleading config that suggests tools that are not actually part of the workflow.
why_good_first_issue: Pure config hygiene: either delete the two unused [tool.*] sections, or (if the team wants spell-checking) wire codespell into the Makefile + dev deps. Easy to scope, low risk, and a good way to learn the project's tooling. ruff already enforces line length, so [tool.black] is redundant.
repro_steps: 1) grep -in 'black\|codespell' pyproject.toml uv.lock -> only the two [tool.*] section headers in pyproject.toml; nothing in uv.lock. 2) grep -rin 'black\|codespell' Makefile .github/ -> no matches (not invoked). 3) Makefile fmt/lint targets call only ruff.
repro_result: CONFIRMED via inspection. pyproject.toml:34 = '[tool.black]', pyproject.toml:61 = '[tool.codespell]'. grep over uv.lock finds neither package (so they are not installed). grep over Makefile and .github/ finds no 'black'/'codespell' invocation. CI (.github/workflows/pull_request.yaml) runs only make static/lint/unit; lint uses ruff (`make lint` => 'All checks passed!' / '6 files already formatted'). The black config (line-length 99) duplicates [tool.ruff] line-length = 99.
fix_hint: Remove the [tool.black] and [tool.codespell] sections from pyproject.toml (ruff already handles formatting and enforces line-length 99). If spell-checking is actually wanted, instead add codespell to [project.optional-dependencies].dev and add a `codespell` invocation to the Makefile lint target + uv.lock.
notes: Self-identified (repo has 0 open issues). Language detected: Python (src/ package observability_charm_tools, pyproject.toml, uv.lock, Makefile). Verified against current main (depth-80 clone). No outbound writes performed.
